* Add script to fix Meta ads with wrong landing URLs (holibob.com + stale subdomains)

Migration scripts used holibob.com as fallback when targetUrl was null, and some
ads have stale -1 suffix subdomains. This script reconstructs correct URLs from
current site/microsite domains and updates both Meta API and DB.

Also hardens ads worker: throws on missing URL, validates Meta token access,
shows actual child ad set URLs in bidding dashboard.

commit.message:
Add in-memory rate limiting on AI-powered API routes (#72)

Fixed-window rate limiter (60s) in middleware for /api/search (100/min),
/api/cpt (50/min), and /api/clarify (50/min). Blocked requests return
429 with Retry-After headers before reaching auth refresh or route
handlers. Zero external dependencies.

Closes #21

All client pages now verify API responses are arrays before calling
.find(), .filter(), .map() etc. Prevents "v.find is not a function"
crashes when an API returns an error object instead of an array.

Affected pages: dashboard, cases list, PRW, skills, profiles,
analysis, and results.

commit.message:
Add null guard to SurvivorAnims AnimSetInt to prevent GetHealth01 crash

When Expansion AI proxy entities flood the engine with GetHealth01 errors
on the client, engine state can become corrupted. The next AnimSetInt call
on the local player's ModCommandHandlerAfter then crashes with an access
violation at a corrupted heap address. Adding a null check on
GetAnimInterface() prevents the crash.

fix(npc): proxy Gemini API via Vercel serverless function

Browser cannot call Gemini API directly (unregistered callers error).

- API key stays server-side only (not exposed in browser)
- Add vercel.json for correct build/output config

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: builder source storage respects per-subtype attribute ownership

Each builder_basket_source subtype owns different attributes in the
schema. Previous code applied all attributes generically, causing
TypeDB 3.x [INF11] type-inference errors when e.g. ecf_source got
'has percentage' (which only cni_source owns).

Added _SOURCE_SUBTYPE_ATTRS map matching schema_unified.tql ownership.
Storage now only sets attributes the concrete subtype actually owns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: correct MariaDB download URL for macOS x64

Fix VUI: correct detectable lift threshold to ~3.9pp per cohort

~600 control clients per cohort means ~3.9pp needed for significance,
not 1.29pp (which was the pooled/overall figure). Even best cohort
(Feb 2026 +3.61pp, p=0.073) falls just short. Increasing control to
15% (~4,200/cohort) brings threshold to ~1.5pp.

fix: enable BFF→API communication in dev via DEV_BYPASS_RBAC

The web BFF was getting 401s from RBAC-protected routes because
RequireRole requires X-User-ID + DB membership, which the BFF
cannot provide without JWT/Cognito integration.

- Add DEV_BYPASS_RBAC=true bypass in RequireRole (injects RoleOwner,
  skips all identity/membership checks when env var is set)
- Set DEV_BYPASS_RBAC=true in compose.override.yaml and .example
- Read API_AUTH_TOKEN from env in web NewAPIClient (falls back to
  "dev-token" when unset)
- Add TestRequireRole/DEV_BYPASS_RBAC test case using t.Setenv
- Add TestNewAPIClient_AuthHeader tests for the token env var logic

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix foto pipeline: 6 breekpunten opgelost van scanner tot products.json

A. github-sync dedup key: platform::name::condition (was platform::name)
   Voorkomt dat dual listings (Goed + Redelijke staat) elkaar overschrijven

B. Image URLs zonder timestamps in products.json
   Strip ?t=xxx bij opslag in add-product + github-sync normalisatie
   Voorkomt stale cached URLs die nooit updaten

C. imageCount afleiden uit werkelijke imageUrls keys
   Niet meer vertrouwen op client-input voor image count

D. v2 cache invalidatie retry bij upload-image
   Als eerste delete faalt, retry 1x — oude cached foto is erger dan 200ms

E. Image existence check bij github-sync
   Valideer dat image blob keys bestaan, null-out ontbrekende images
   Producten zonder werkende foto krijgen null zodat shop geen 404 toont

F. product-image Sharp fallback
   Als Sharp optimalisatie crasht, serveer origineel (beter dan 404)

fix(theme): restore neutral dark/light — achromatic grays, visible orbs, frosted blue glass

Neutral dark was near-black (L=0.13) since dbad1f6 imported playground
tokens verbatim. This restores dark gray (L=0.27) with achromatic grays
(C=0), differentiates --secondary from --background, adds cold blue tint
to glass surfaces, makes ambient orbs visible in both modes, and fixes
h1 title color + AnimationsToggle button order.

Changes:
- globals.css: neutral dark tokens raised to L=0.27, achromatic (C=0)
- globals.css: neutral light glass opacity reduced (0.55→0.35) for airiness
- globals.css: cold blue glass-tint (C=0.015 H=250) both modes
- globals.css: ambient orb tokens for both dark and light modes
- globals.css: body bg + transparent page-background so orbs show through
- page.tsx: add text-foreground to h1 (was black in neutral)
- page.tsx: move AnimationsToggle before ThemeSwitcher (layout stability)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(encryption): correctly parse master_key_configured from encrypt-decrypt response

checkEncryptionStatus was discarding the top-level master_key_configured
field and only returning encryption_status (which is {} on a key-only probe).
The screen then looked for the wrong key name ('key_configured') and fell
back to isNotEmpty, which is false on an empty map — always showing the
error banner even when the key is set.

Fix: inject master_key_configured into the returned map in the service,
and read that key in the screen.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add intent-based matchmaking system with AI creation and generic connections

Features:
- Intent creation with dual modes: AI-powered natural language parsing (OpenAI GPT-4) and traditional form
- Dashboard-centric intent management with IntentSection across all profile types
- Generic connection system supporting all profile types (Startup ↔ Individual, Partner, Investor)
- Intent-based matching algorithm with 4-dimensional scoring (40pts intent, 30pts profile, 20pts budget, 10pts availability)
- IntentMatchesModal displaying scored matches in dashboard
- Connection request/accept/decline flow for all profile types
- Approval pending modal shown after onboarding completion (auto-closes after 4s)

Backend:
- Updated Connection model with generic from_user_id/to_user_id fields
- Added Intent model with metadata and status tracking
- New storage methods: createGenericConnection, getConnectionsByUser, acceptConnection, declineConnection
- AI intent parsing endpoint using OpenAI GPT-4
- Intent CRUD and matching endpoints

Frontend:
- Created 8 new intent components (IntentCard, IntentSection, AIIntentCreator, FormIntentCreator, etc.)
- Added ApprovalPendingModal with animations
- Integrated IntentSection into all dashboard types
- use-intents hook for React Query-based state management

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: only persist API keys in config.env, not settings

Previously setup_api_keys() wrote MODEL_NAME, MAX_ITERATIONS,
TEMPERATURE, etc. to ~/.webresearch/config.env, freezing them at
install time and causing stale model names after package upgrades.

Now config.env stores only GEMINI_API_KEY and SERPER_API_KEY.
All other settings always come from webresearch/config.py defaults,
so they stay current across upgrades without requiring re-setup.
